meta.title: The Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: A deterministic end-of-day reflection tool across three psychological axes: Locus (Victim vs Victor), Orientation (Contribution vs Entitlement), and Radius (Self-Centric vs Altrocentric).

nodes.text: Good evening. Before the day closes, let's spend a few minutes with it. No right answers — just honest ones.
If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Stormy — things kept going wrong
Overcast — nothing terrible, nothing great
Partly cloudy — mixed, but I managed
Clear — I felt on top of things
answer=Stormy — things kept going wrong:A1_Q_HARD
answer=Overcast — nothing terrible, nothing great:A1_Q_DRIFT
answer=Partly cloudy — mixed, but I managed:A1_Q_NAVIGATE
answer=Clear — I felt on top of things:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH

nodes.text: You said it was stormy. When something went sideways today, what was your first instinct?
options: Look for what I could still control
Feel frustrated — this shouldn't be happening
Figure out who dropped the ball
Push through it alone, even if it hurt

nodes.text: Overcast days can be deceptive — nothing dramatic, but were you steering, or just drifting?
options: I steered — I made deliberate choices
I drifted — just got through it
I avoided something I should have faced
I showed up but didn't fully engage

nodes.text: Mixed days have decision points. When the clouds came in, what did you do?
options: I found a different route
I waited to see if things cleared up
I asked for help and kept moving
I complained but eventually adapted

nodes.text: A clear day — what made it clear? When something went well today, what was the main reason?
options: I prepared well or made a good call
The team came through
Things just lined up — I got lucky
I adapted fast when it mattered
parent=A1_Q_HARD,answer=Look for what I could still control:A1_R_PIVOT
parent=A1_Q_HARD,answer=Feel frustrated — this shouldn't be happening:A1_R_EXT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_HARD,answer=Figure out who dropped the ball:A1_R_EXT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_HARD,answer=Push through it alone, even if it hurt:A1_R_STOIC
parent=A1_Q_DRIFT,answer=I steered — I made deliberate choices:A1_R_SUBTLE_AGENCY
parent=A1_Q_DRIFT,answer=I drifted — just got through it:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE
parent=A1_Q_DRIFT,answer=I avoided something I should have faced:A1_R_AVOIDANCE
parent=A1_Q_DRIFT,answer=I showed up but didn't fully engage:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE
parent=A1_Q_NAVIGATE,answer=I found a different route:A1_R_INT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_NAVIGATE,answer=I waited to see if things cleared up:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE
parent=A1_Q_NAVIGATE,answer=I asked for help and kept moving:A1_R_INT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_NAVIGATE,answer=I complained but eventually adapted:A1_R_STOIC
parent=A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH,answer=I prepared well or made a good call:A1_R_INT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH,answer=The team came through:A1_R_INT_STRONG
parent=A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH,answer=Things just lined up — I got lucky:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE
parent=A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH,answer=I adapted fast when it mattered:A1_R_INT_STRONG

nodes.text: You stayed in the driver's seat today — not because everything went perfectly, but because you kept looking for what you could do. That's not something that happens to you. That's something you chose.

When things go wrong, the mind looks outward first — for explanations, for someone to hold accountable. That's human. But in the middle of all that, there was at least one moment where you had a choice. It might have been small. What was it?

Some days, the current carries us. That's fine — you can't be intentional every hour. But notice: even in a drifting day, you made calls. You chose what to respond to, what to let go. Agency doesn't have to be loud.
Something hard hit you today — and your first move was to look for what you still controlled. That's not a small thing. That instinct is the whole game.

You pushed through — and there's real grit in that. Worth noticing, though: pushing through alone and navigating intelligently are different moves. One costs more. Both can be right, depending on the situation.
You called it overcast, but you steered. There's something quiet and underrated about a day where you made deliberate choices without drama. That's a kind of mastery.
You noticed the avoidance — which is itself a form of agency. Knowing you sidestepped something is the first step toward deciding whether to go back for it.
Now let's shift lenses. From how you handled things — to what you gave.
Think about one interaction you had today — with a colleague, a manager, a report. Which of these comes closest to what happened?
options: I helped someone who didn't ask me to
I did what was expected, nothing more
I felt like my effort wasn't recognized
I went beyond my role to move something forward
answer=I helped someone who didn't ask me to:A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH
answer=I did what was expected, nothing more:A2_Q_NEUTRAL
answer=I felt like my effort wasn't recognized:A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT
answer=I went beyond my role to move something forward:A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH

nodes.text: You gave something extra today. When you did that, what was driving it?
options: I wanted to — it felt right
I hoped it would be noticed
It needed doing and I was there
I wanted that person to succeed

nodes.text: You met the bar — which is real. But was there a moment today where you could have given more and chose not to?
options: Yes — I held back deliberately
Yes — I was too depleted to give more
No — I genuinely gave all I had
I didn't see an opportunity to do more

nodes.text: The recognition gap stings. When you think about what you gave today versus what came back — be honest: what were you hoping for?
options: A thank-you or acknowledgment
Credit for something I initiated
To be included in the decision
Nothing specific — it just felt unbalanced
parent=A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH,answer=I wanted to — it felt right:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
parent=A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH,answer=I hoped it would be noticed:A2_R_CONTRIB_MIXED
parent=A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH,answer=It needed doing and I was there:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
parent=A2_Q_CONTRIB_HIGH,answer=I wanted that person to succeed:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
parent=A2_Q_NEUTRAL,answer=Yes — I held back deliberately:A2_R_CONSERVATION
parent=A2_Q_NEUTRAL,answer=Yes — I was too depleted to give more:A2_R_DEPLETION
parent=A2_Q_NEUTRAL,answer=No — I genuinely gave all I had:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
parent=A2_Q_NEUTRAL,answer=I didn't see an opportunity to do more:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
parent=A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT,answer=A thank-you or acknowledgment:A2_R_ENT_GENTLE
parent=A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT,answer=Credit for something I initiated:A2_R_ENT_MODERATE
parent=A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT,answer=To be included in the decision:A2_R_ENT_GENTLE
parent=A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT,answer=Nothing specific — it just felt unbalanced:A2_R_ENT_GENTLE

nodes.text: You gave because you wanted to — not because you were keeping score. That's contribution at its cleanest. It's also rare. Most people's generosity at work has a quiet ledger running underneath it.

You gave extra today, and you also wanted it to land. That's not a flaw — wanting to matter is human. Worth noticing, though: the giving happened regardless of whether the recognition did. That's worth something.

The recognition question is interesting. Sometimes what looks like entitlement is really just a legitimate need for feedback — to know the work landed. Before writing off the feeling, ask: is this about deserving, or about knowing you made a difference?

Credit matters — especially when you took the initiative. But there's a difference between 'I want to know this was seen' and 'I need to be acknowledged to feel okay about my work.' The first is healthy. The second is worth watching.
You had more to give and you chose not to. That could be self-protection, or it could be a quiet way of withdrawing. Only you know which. Both are okay — but it's worth naming which one it was.
Running on empty isn't a character flaw — it's information. You gave what you had. A depleted day isn't a selfish day. Take note of what drained you, because that's what needs attention.
One more shift. From what you gave — to who you were thinking about.
When you think about today's biggest challenge or moment — who was in the frame?
options: Just me — it was my problem, my stress
Me and one other person — a specific colleague
My whole team — we were all in it
The person or people we ultimately serve
answer=Just me — it was my problem, my stress:A3_Q_SELF
answer=Me and one other person — a specific colleague:A3_Q_DYADIC
answer=My whole team — we were all in it:A3_Q_TEAM
answer=The person or people we ultimately serve:A3_Q_BEYOND

nodes.text: You were in the frame alone. Was anyone else affected by what you were going through — even if you weren't thinking about them in the moment?
options: Probably yes, but I didn't have bandwidth to notice
No — this was genuinely just about me
Yes, and I felt guilty about that
Yes — and I tried to protect them from it

nodes.text: You had one specific person in mind. What were you feeling toward them?
options: Concern — I was thinking about how they were doing
Frustration — they were part of the problem
Gratitude — they helped me get through it
Responsibility — I needed to make sure they were okay

nodes.text: The whole team was in it together. In that shared struggle, where did your attention tend to go?
options: My own contribution — did I pull my weight?
The weakest link — who needed more support?
The goal — are we going to get there?
The dynamic — how is this affecting us as a unit?

nodes.text: You kept the end user or customer in mind — that's rare. What does that mean to how you approach your work?
options: It grounds me — when internal stuff gets heavy, it refocuses me
It's just how I think — I don't know any other way
It's something I'm working toward — I'm getting better at it
Today was unusual — I don't usually think that broadly
parent=A3_Q_SELF,answer=Probably yes, but I didn't have bandwidth to notice:A3_R_SELF_AWARE
parent=A3_Q_SELF,answer=No — this was genuinely just about me:A3_R_SELF_PURE
parent=A3_Q_SELF,answer=Yes, and I felt guilty about that:A3_R_SELF_GUILT
parent=A3_Q_SELF,answer=Yes — and I tried to protect them from it:A3_R_SELF_PROTECT
parent=A3_Q_DYADIC,answer=Concern — I was thinking about how they were doing:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_DYADIC,answer=Frustration — they were part of the problem:A3_R_ALTRO_FRICTION
parent=A3_Q_DYADIC,answer=Gratitude — they helped me get through it:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_DYADIC,answer=Responsibility — I needed to make sure they were okay:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_TEAM,answer=My own contribution — did I pull my weight?:A3_R_SELF_AWARE
parent=A3_Q_TEAM,answer=The weakest link — who needed more support?:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_TEAM,answer=The goal — are we going to get there?:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_TEAM,answer=The dynamic — how is this affecting us as a unit?:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_BEYOND,answer=It grounds me — when internal stuff gets heavy, it refocuses me:A3_R_TRANSCEND
parent=A3_Q_BEYOND,answer=It's just how I think — I don't know any other way:A3_R_TRANSCEND
parent=A3_Q_BEYOND,answer=It's something I'm working toward — I'm getting better at it:A3_R_ALTRO_HIGH
parent=A3_Q_BEYOND,answer=Today was unusual — I don't usually think that broadly:A3_R_SELF_AWARE

nodes.text: Sometimes a challenge really is just yours — that's not selfishness, that's honest. No one has an unlimited radius of concern. But it's worth asking tomorrow: is there one person in my orbit I could notice more?

You were narrowly focused — and you know it. That self-awareness is the opening. The bandwidth to notice others doesn't come from having an easy day; it comes from practice. Even one small glance outward changes the texture of a hard day.

Guilt about self-focus is itself a form of other-awareness — you noticed. But guilt isn't the goal. Try something lighter: curiosity. Next time you're in a hard moment, just ask once: how is the person next to me doing right now?
You absorbed something difficult so others wouldn't have to. That's not self-centeredness — that's a form of care. The distinction matters: you were in the frame alone, but others were the reason.
Today you held other people in mind — their challenges, their progress, their wellbeing. That kind of outward attention isn't separate from your own performance. It usually is your performance.

Frustration with someone still means they were in your field of view. The question worth asking is: what was going on for them today? Not to excuse anything — but understanding someone's position often changes what's possible next.

Keeping the end user in mind — especially when the day gets heavy — is one of the harder habits to build. When you can zoom out to that frame, the internal noise usually gets quieter. What you do becomes part of something bigger than how it felt.
Today you called it '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. You leaned {axis1.dominant} on agency, {axis2.dominant} on contribution, and your concern extended {axis3.dominant}. {summary_reflection}

summary_templates.internal_contribution_altrocentric: You showed up with ownership, gave generously, and kept others in mind. Days like this compound — they make you someone people rely on.
summary_templates.internal_contribution_self: You owned your day and contributed — both real. Tomorrow, one small move: notice someone else's day.
summary_templates.internal_entitlement_altrocentric: You handled today well and kept others in mind — but some part of you was tracking the score. That tension is worth sitting with.
summary_templates.internal_entitlement_self: You drove your own day — that's solid. The edge for tomorrow: shift from what the day gave you to what you gave the day.
summary_templates.external_contribution_altrocentric: You gave today, and you held others in mind — even while feeling like things were out of your control. That generosity under pressure is harder than it looks.
summary_templates.external_contribution_self: You contributed — that counts. The awareness that you're not fully in the driver's seat is honest. Start with one small moment tomorrow where you choose the response.
summary_templates.external_entitlement_self: Today felt like things happened to you, and the return on your effort felt thin. That's a real feeling. But it's also a loop — and loops can be interrupted.
summary_templates.external_entitlement_altrocentric: A harder day. You're watching what's owed while also keeping others in view — that combination creates strain. Start tomorrow with one thing you chose.

nodes.text: See you tomorrow.
